updated_triples.subject: ent-456
updated_triples.note: MoU status upgrade: 'dead' (6/11) → 'denied' (6/12 Iran) → 'never been closer' (6/13 Iran FM). Dramatic 48-hour reversal.

updated_triples.subject: ent-031
updated_triples.note: Pakistan PM Sharif central mediator — declares final text after direct coordination with US and Iran

inferred_triples.subject: ent-578
inferred_triples.note: IRGC first positive signal (Fars 'high probability') vs IRGC 2nd night attacks (6/12) = 24-hour reversal from military escalation to diplomatic acceptance. Indicates IRGC internal deliberation may have concluded, or dual-track IRGC behavior (military pressure while accepting political outcome)

inferred_triples.subject: ent-575
inferred_triples.note: Pakistan 'final text' + Iran FM 'never been closer' = convergent positive signals from two independent parties. However, Tasnim 'requires review' and Vance 'TBD' introduce divergence — multi-party messaging NOT aligned

inferred_triples.subject: ent-580
inferred_triples.note: Trump deal claim (6/12) → Pakistan final text (6/13) → Oil crash to $88. 2-day oil decline chain: Brent $93→$90.38→$88. Market pricing in deal completion at increasing probability. Fitch $70 scenario = full de-escalation pricing.

inferred_triples.subject: ent-582
inferred_triples.note: Netanyahu 'not a party' distancing from MoU while simultaneously continuing Lebanon strikes (12+ killed). Hedging: if deal fails, Israel maintains military initiative. If deal succeeds, Israel claims non-participation to avoid constraints.

inferred_triples.subject: ent-583
inferred_triples.note: Vance 'still TBD' vs US official 80-85% confidence = intra-administration messaging divergence. Vance may be providing negotiating leverage (dampening expectations) while officials signal confidence. Classic good-cop/bad-cop within same administration.
